route.name: Salmon River Solitude
route.description: Public GPX remote Idaho river/backcountry route with gravel, dirt roads, climbing, and sparse service logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX remote Idaho river/backcountry route with gravel, dirt roads, climbing, and sparse service logistics.
route.bestSeason: Summer/fall windows require heat, smoke, water, and road checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX remote Idaho river/backcountry route with gravel, dirt roads, climbing, and sparse service logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water likely near river corridors, but treatment and dry gaps need measurement.
resources.food: Sparse resupply; carry food and verify any services.
resources.camping: River/backcountry camp options must be selected and verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until services/camps are verified.
Carry conservative repair and water capacity.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Active South Fork Salmon River Road Repair Closure Order 0412-636 is in the route corridor and has not been proven off-route by GPX/closure-map overlay.
No exact legal overnight plan with date-confirmed availability for July 10, July 11, and July 12.
Potable-water outages and no-potable-water campgrounds require a rebuilt water plan based on verified natural sources and carry capacity.
Food/resupply in Yellow Pine, Big Creek, Warren, and Secesh is too limited to rely on without calls.
Bear/food-storage compliance method was not proven from a route-specific official source.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Overlay the GPX against Payette Order 0412-636 and reject/reroute if any segment uses the closed NFS Road 50674 miles 9.7-13.5.
Choose exact overnight coordinates or bookable lodging for July 10, July 11, and July 12; verify official land status, closure status, and date availability.
Call McCall and Krassel Ranger Districts to confirm dispersed-camp legality and food-storage requirements for the selected camps.
Call all route resupply businesses before relying on meals, groceries, or fuel.
Refresh Payette alerts, fire restrictions, NWS forecast, AirNow/Idaho smoke, and active incidents on July 9 evening or July 10 morning.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Remote Idaho route needs heat, smoke, water, camp, and river-corridor access checks.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.

days.name: Salmon River Solitude segment 1
days.camp: River/backcountry camp options must be selected and verified.

waypoints.name: Confluence of S and E Forks of Salmon River

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Do not rely on Big Creek potable water; plan to filter and treat natural sources and carry enough for the Warren Summit dry/exposed climb.

readinessEvidence.finding: Public route page and GPX download exist; local exported GPX is present.
Payette alerts show an active South Fork Salmon River Road Repair Closure, Order 0412-636, effective January 9 through December 31, 2026; the GPX has not been proven outside the closure segment.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Payette National Forest South Fork Salmon River Road Repair Closure

readinessEvidence.finding: Key developed camps are first-come-first-served and no exact July 10-12 legal overnight itinerary with confirmed availability or verified dispersed coordinates was proven.
Big Creek potable water is out of service; Buckhorn Bar potable water was not functioning as of the source note; Camp Creek and Deadman Bar have no potable water.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Payette National Forest campground water notices

readinessEvidence.finding: Fire danger, closures, road conditions, thunderstorms, and smoke cannot be locked from July 2 for July 10-13; final refresh is mandatory.
